law.text:
Chapter 4. Succession rights of the spouse and the legal cohabitant

 Section 1. The surviving spouse

 Art. 4.17. Succession rights of the surviving spouse
 par. 1. When the deceased leaves descendants, adopted children, or descendants of these, the surviving spouse acquires the usufruct of the entire estate.
 par. 2. When the deceased leaves relatives in the ascending line or close collateral relatives, the surviving spouse acquires:
 (1) full ownership of the share of the first deceased in the joint property and in the property held exclusively undivided between the spouses, and
 (2) usufruct of the other goods of their own property.
 (3) usufruct of the goods subject to the right of legal reversion referred to in article 4.24 or in article 4.25, unless otherwise stipulated in the deed of donation or in the will.
 par. 3. When the deceased leaves other heirs or no heirs, the surviving spouse acquires full ownership of the entire estate.

 Art. 4.18. Usufruct of donated goods
 Upon the death of the donor, the surviving spouse who is part of the estate receives the usufruct of the goods that the donor has given and of which he reserved the usufruct, provided that the spouse already held that capacity at the time of the donation and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until his death.
 Upon the death of the donor, the surviving spouse who is part of the estate receives the usufruct of the real estate that served as the family’s main residence at the opening of the estate and the household effects therein, provided that the donor reserved the usufruct of those goods at the time of donation, that the spouse legally cohabited with the donor at the time of donation, and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until his death.
 The spouse may renounce that usufruct. Articles 4.244 to 4.253 apply to such renunciation when it takes place during the lifetime of the donor.

 Art. 4.19. Exercise of succession usufruct
 When the surviving spouse is entitled to the usufruct of the entire estate, that usufruct is established as stipulated in the following paragraphs.
 The usufruct referred to in the first paragraph encumbers the goods present on the day of death, after deduction of legacies.
 It also encumbers:
 (1) under the conditions specified in article 4.18, the goods donated by the deceased of which he reserved the usufruct;
 (2) under the conditions and according to the modalities determined in title 2, subtitle 3, chapter 2, and notwithstanding the second paragraph, the goods bequeathed by the deceased insofar as the surviving spouse can request or enjoy the reduction thereof;
 (3) under the conditions and pursuant to the further rules stipulated in title 2, subtitle 3, chapter 2, the goods donated by the deceased insofar as the surviving spouse can request or enjoy the reduction thereof.

 Art. 4.20. Right to rent
 The surviving spouse obtains alone, to the exclusion of all other heirs, the right to the rent of the real estate that served as the family’s main residence at the opening of the deceased’s estate.

 Art. 4.21. Inventory and investment
 Notwithstanding any other provision, anyone who obtains bare ownership may demand that an inventory of the movable goods and a statement of the immovable goods encumbered by usufruct be drawn up, and that the sums of money be invested.

 Art. 4.22. Exclusion or forfeiture
 par. 1. The surviving spouse may be wholly or partially excluded from their succession rights or declared to have forfeited them if they have been wholly or partially deprived of parental authority over the children born of their marriage with the deceased.
 par. 2. The legal action is brought by the descendants within one year, calculated either from the opening of the estate or from the deprivation of parental authority.
 The judgment takes effect as of the date on which the claim was instituted.
 par. 3. If the usufruct has already been converted into full ownership of a good or into a sum of money, or if the bare ownership of the goods referred to in article 4.61, paragraph 7, has already been transferred, the exclusion or forfeiture entitles to compensation.
 The compensation is determined by the court and corresponds to the value of the usufruct, taking into account the probable lifespan of the usufructuary at the time of instituting the legal action.
 If the usufruct has been converted into an annuity, the judgment operates retroactively to the same date.

 Section 2. The surviving legal cohabitant

 Art. 4.23. Succession rights and right to rent of the surviving legal cohabitant
 par. 1. The surviving legal cohabitant acquires, with whichever heirs they share the estate, the usufruct of the real estate that served as the family’s main residence at the opening of the deceased’s estate, and of the household effects therein.
 This right is also exercised by the surviving legal cohabitant if the goods mentioned in the first paragraph are subject to a right of legal reversion referred to in article 4.24 or in article 4.25.
 par. 2. The surviving legal cohabitant who is part of the estate receives, upon the donor’s death, the usufruct of the real estate that at the opening of the estate served as the family’s main residence and of the household effects therein, if the donor reserved the usufruct of these goods at the time of donation, provided that the legal cohabitant held that status at the time of the donation and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until their death.
 The legal cohabitant may renounce the usufruct referred to in the first paragraph. Articles 4.244 to 4.253 apply to such renunciation when it occurs during the lifetime of the donor.
 par. 3. The surviving legal cohabitant acquires alone, excluding all other heirs, the right to the rent of the real estate that served as the family’s main residence at the opening of the estate of the previously deceased legal cohabitant, and acquires the usufruct of the household effects therein.
 par. 4. The preceding provisions do not apply when the surviving legal cohabitant is a descendant of the previously deceased legal cohabitant.
 par. 5. Notwithstanding any other provision, anyone who obtains bare ownership may demand that an inventory of the household effects and a statement of the real estate that served as the family’s main residence be drawn up.
 par. 6. The rules regarding the usufruct of the surviving spouse set out in articles 4.22 and 4.60 to 4.64 apply accordingly to the usufruct of the surviving legal cohabitant.

kb_used.fo:
vocabulary V {
  type Person
  type Estate
  type Good
  type RealEstate
  type HouseholdGoods
  type LegalAction
  type Judgment
  type Donation
  type Will
  type DeedOfGift
  type Time
  IsSurvivingSpouse: Person -> Bool
  IsDeceased: Person -> Bool
  LeavesDescendants: Person -> Bool
  LeavesAdoptedChildren: Person -> Bool
  LeavesDescendantsOfDescendants: Person -> Bool
  LeavesRelativesInAscendingLine: Person -> Bool
  LeavesCloseCollateralRelatives: Person -> Bool
  LeavesOtherHeirs: Person -> Bool
  LeavesNoHeirs: Person -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate: Person * Estate -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseAcquiresInheritanceRights: Person * Estate -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseHasFullOwnershipPortionFirstDeceased: Person * Good -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseHasUsufructOnOtherGoods: Person * Good -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseHasUsufructOnGoodsWithLegalReversion: Person * Good -> Bool
  IsUsufructReservedByDeceased: Good -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseHadStatusAtTimeOfDonation: Person * Time -> Bool
  DeceasedHeldUsufructUntilDeath: Person * Time -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseMayRenounceUsufruct: Person * Good -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseAcquiresFullOwnershipEntireEstate: Person * Estate -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseAcquiresUsufructOfDonatedGoodsWithReservedUsufruct: Person * Good -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseAcquiresUsufructOfMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods: Person * RealEstate -> Bool
  GoodsEncumberedByUsufruct: Good * Person -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseAcquiresRightToRentMainResidence: Person * RealEstate -> Bool
  AcquiresNakedOwnership: Person * Good -> Bool
  MayDemandInventoryAndStatement: Person * Good -> Bool
  SurvivingSpouseDeprivedOfParentalAuthority: Person -> Bool
  IsExcludedOrForfeitedFromInheritanceRights: Person -> Bool
  LegalActionInitiatedByDescendants: LegalAction * Person -> Bool
  LegalActionWithinOneYearFromOpeningOrDeprivation: LegalAction * Time -> Bool
  JudgmentTakesEffectFromClaimDate: Judgment * Time -> Bool
  UsufructConvertedToOwnershipOrSum: Good -> Bool
  CompensationDeterminedByCourt: Judgment * Person -> Bool
  UsufructConvertedToAnnuity: Good -> Bool
  JudgmentHasRetroactiveEffect: Judgment * Time -> Bool
  IsSurvivingStatutoryCohabitant: Person -> Bool
  SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods: Person * RealEstate -> Bool
  SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantInheritsWithOthers: Person -> Bool
  GoodsSubjectToLegalReversion: Good -> Bool
  SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantHadStatusAtTimeOfDonation: Person * Time -> Bool
  SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantMayRenounceUsufruct: Person * Good -> Bool
  SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresRightToRentMainResidence: Person * RealEstate -> Bool
  SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantIsDescendant: Person -> Bool
  UsufructRulesApplyMutatisMutandis: Person -> Bool}

theory T:V {
  ! x in Person, e in Estate: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & LeavesDescendants(x) & IsDeceased(x)) => (SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate(x,e)).
  ! x in Person, e in Estate: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & LeavesAdoptedChildren(x) & IsDeceased(x)) => (SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate(x,e)).
  ! x in Person, e in Estate: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & LeavesDescendantsOfDescendants(x) & IsDeceased(x)) => (SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate(x,e)).
  ! x in Person, e in Estate: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & LeavesRelativesInAscendingLine(x) & IsDeceased(x)) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresInheritanceRights(x,e)).
  ! x in Person, e in Estate: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & LeavesCloseCollateralRelatives(x) & IsDeceased(x)) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresInheritanceRights(x,e)).
  ! x in Person, g in Good, e in Estate: (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresInheritanceRights(x,e)) => (SurvivingSpouseHasFullOwnershipPortionFirstDeceased(x,g) & SurvivingSpouseHasUsufructOnOtherGoods(x,g) & SurvivingSpouseHasUsufructOnGoodsWithLegalReversion(x,g)).
  ! x in Person, e in Estate: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & LeavesOtherHeirs(x) & IsDeceased(x)) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresFullOwnershipEntireEstate(x,e)).
  ! x in Person, e in Estate: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & LeavesNoHeirs(x) & IsDeceased(x)) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresFullOwnershipEntireEstate(x,e)).
  ! x in Person, g in Good, t in Time: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & SurvivingSpouseHadStatusAtTimeOfDonation(x,t) & DeceasedHeldUsufructUntilDeath(x,t) & IsUsufructReservedByDeceased(g)) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresUsufructOfDonatedGoodsWithReservedUsufruct(x,g)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate, t in Time, g in Good: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & IsUsufructReservedByDeceased(g) & SurvivingSpouseHadStatusAtTimeOfDonation(x,t) & DeceasedHeldUsufructUntilDeath(x,t)) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresUsufructOfMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)).
  ! x in Person, g in Good: (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresUsufructOfDonatedGoodsWithReservedUsufruct(x,g)) => (SurvivingSpouseMayRenounceUsufruct(x,g)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate, g in Good: (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresUsufructOfMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)) => (SurvivingSpouseMayRenounceUsufruct(x,g)).
  ! x in Person, e in Estate, g in Good: (SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate(x,e)) => (GoodsEncumberedByUsufruct(g,x)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate, e in Estate, g in Good: (SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate(x,e)) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresUsufructOfDonatedGoodsWithReservedUsufruct(x,g)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate: (true) => (SurvivingSpouseAcquiresRightToRentMainResidence(x,r)).
  ! x in Person, y in Person, g in Good: (AcquiresNakedOwnership(x,g)) => (MayDemandInventoryAndStatement(x,g)).
  ! x in Person: (IsSurvivingSpouse(x) & SurvivingSpouseDeprivedOfParentalAuthority(x)) => (IsExcludedOrForfeitedFromInheritanceRights(x)).
  ! a in LegalAction, x in Person, t in Time: (LegalActionInitiatedByDescendants(a,x) & LegalActionWithinOneYearFromOpeningOrDeprivation(a,t)) => (IsExcludedOrForfeitedFromInheritanceRights(x)).
  ! j in Judgment, t in Time: (JudgmentTakesEffectFromClaimDate(j,t)) => (JudgmentHasRetroactiveEffect(j,t)).
  ! g in Good, j in Judgment, x in Person: (UsufructConvertedToOwnershipOrSum(g) & CompensationDeterminedByCourt(j,x)) => (CompensationDeterminedByCourt(j,x)).
  ! g in Good, j in Judgment, t in Time: (UsufructConvertedToAnnuity(g) & JudgmentHasRetroactiveEffect(j,t)) => (JudgmentHasRetroactiveEffect(j,t)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate, t in Time, g in Good: (IsSurvivingStatutoryCohabitant(x) & SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantInheritsWithOthers(x) & IsUsufructReservedByDeceased(g) & SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantHadStatusAtTimeOfDonation(x,t) & DeceasedHeldUsufructUntilDeath(x,t)) => (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)).
  ! x in Person, g in Good, r in RealEstate: (GoodsSubjectToLegalReversion(g)) => (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate, t in Time, g in Good: (IsSurvivingStatutoryCohabitant(x) & IsUsufructReservedByDeceased(g) & SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantHadStatusAtTimeOfDonation(x,t) & DeceasedHeldUsufructUntilDeath(x,t)) => (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)).
  ! x in Person, g in Good, r in RealEstate: (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)) => (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantMayRenounceUsufruct(x,g)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate, g in Good: (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)) => (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantMayRenounceUsufruct(x,g)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate: (IsSurvivingStatutoryCohabitant(x) & ~SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantIsDescendant(x)) => (SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresRightToRentMainResidence(x,r) & SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)).
  ! x in Person, r in RealEstate: (IsSurvivingStatutoryCohabitant(x) & SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantIsDescendant(x)) => (~SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresRightToRentMainResidence(x,r) & ~SurvivingStatutoryCohabitantAcquiresUsufructMainResidenceAndHouseholdGoods(x,r)).
  ! x in Person, y in Person, g in Good: (AcquiresNakedOwnership(x,g)) => (MayDemandInventoryAndStatement(x,g)).
  ! x in Person: (UsufructRulesApplyMutatisMutandis(x)) => (IsSurvivingStatutoryCohabitant(x)).
}

case.text: Anna and Bert were married. Bert dies and leaves behind two minor children. There is no will. Anna is still alive.

questions.text: Does the surviving spouse have the right to usufruct of the entire estate according to article 4.17?

symbolic_result.constraint: ? x0 in Person, x1 in Estate: __sel0(x0) & SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate(x0,x1)

pipeline.natural_language: No. Anna does not satisfy SurvivingSpouseHasRightToUsufructOfEntireEstate. (If you expected Yes, ensure case facts use the exact predicate names from the KB schema.)
